name: k8s-helm-chart
description: A Helm chart diff (Chart.yaml + values.yaml + templated Deployment + _helpers.tpl) activates the k8s profile, helm-checklist, and reliability-checklist.
prompt: I've staged the following changes. Run `/kk:review-code` against them.
trap: Model doesn't recognize templates as Kubernetes manifests because `{{ }}` placeholders make them technically invalid YAML, or misses the `values.yaml` / `Chart.yaml` adjacency rule, or spuriously loads `kustomize-checklist.md` alongside helm.

assertions.text: Identifies `k8s` as the active profile — via the `Chart.yaml` filename signal (Helm chart root).
Recognizes `values.yaml` as a Helm values file by filename-adjacency to `Chart.yaml` in the same directory.
Recognizes `templates/deployment.yaml` and `templates/_helpers.tpl` as Helm templates (`.yaml`/`.tpl` under a `templates/` directory whose ancestor contains `Chart.yaml`).
Loads the four always-load checklists from `profiles/k8s/review-code/`.
Loads `reliability-checklist.md` because the template declares a literal `kind: Deployment`.

Emits at least one content-level finding from the fixture — candidates include the mutable `image.tag: latest` in `values.yaml`, the missing `Chart.lock` despite a pinned `postgresql` dependency in `Chart.yaml`, missing `resources` block (`.Values.resources | default {}` yields an empty block when values don't set it), or missing `securityContext` on the templated container. A model that routes correctly but emits zero content-level findings against this fixture does NOT satisfy this eval.
